rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into implementation scopes when binding implementation-level signal routing via assign statements.
Preserve AADL architecture by not introducing new ports, types, or properties in AGREE annexes.
generic_rules: Implementation-level contracts should use assign for output bindings, not guarantees.
Component type contracts must not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes use `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax with assign, eq, const, assume, guarantee as valid clauses.
Only use visible features or declared variables in AGREE expressions—never inferred or internal subcomponent paths.
anti_patterns: Do not encode AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants.
Avoid inventing declarations to resolve unresolved references—use only existing visible identifiers.
irrelevant_topics: assume/guarantee placement in component types for abstract logic
AGREE annex fusion or semantic alignment across models
